author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice
book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice.
The Canons of the CCXVII Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage. Commonly Called The Code of Canons of the African Church.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Synods of Sardica, Carthage, Constantinople, and Carthage Under St. Cyprian, Which Canons Were Received by the Council in Trullo and Ratified by II. Nice

title: Canon XVI.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: That no bishop, presbyter or deacon should be a “conductor;” and that Readers should take wives; and that the clergy should abstain from usury; and at what age they or virgins should be consecrated.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Likewise it seemed good that bishops, presbyters, and deacons should not be “conductors” or “procurators;” nor seek their food by any base and vile business, for they should remember how it is written, “No man fighting for God cumbereth himself with worldly affairs.”

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Also it seemed good that Readers when they come to years of puberty, should be compelled either to take wives or else to profess continence.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Likewise it seemed good that if a clergyman had lent money he should get it back again, but if kind (speciem) he should receive back the same kind as he gave.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And that younger than twenty-five years deacons should not be ordained, nor virgins consecrated.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And that readers should not salute the people.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: A bishop, presbyter, and deacon may not be a “conductor” or a “procurator.”

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: A reader when he comes to puberty must contract marriage or profess continence.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: A cleric who has lent to someone, what he gave let him receive, or as much.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Let not him be a deacon, who is made a deacon being under twenty-five.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: And let not readers salute the people.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: This canon is made up of Canons xv., xviij., and xxj., and added to these Canon j. of the same Second Series of the synod of Hippo, a.d.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Zonaras says this was never observed anywhere but in Africa.

verses.book: The Canons of the 217 Blessed Fathers who assembled at Carthage.
verses.text: Du Pin turns the Latin, saluto, by “addressing his speech to the people.”
